items.title: In Its Latest Act of Repression, Hong Kong Convicted My Father for My Activism
items.description: My father’s conviction is Beijing’s litmus test for Washington: Will the U.S. stand firm in favor of freedom?

items.title: Trump Busts Another Taboo
items.description: It used to be that U.S. presidents didn’t pop off on every Tom, Dick, and Harry.

items.title: A Congressman’s Five Questions for Munich
items.description: This week’s Munich Security Conference comes at a consequential moment for both U.S. security planning and the future of the Western alliance.

items.title: Virginia’s Gerrymandering Vandalism
items.description: The Virginia gerrymander would create an extreme deep-blue House caucus in a purple state based upon winning one election cycle.

items.title: High Fashion Wasn’t Easy in Imperial Spain
items.description: Fabrics, jewelry, and tailoring were tops, but the Hispanic Society shows us that looking good could be hazardous to your health.
